# UC-22.2.59 · NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) — Splunk SOAR + ISE Mean-Time-to-Contain (MTTC) KPI Evidence

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance

*We continuously evidence NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) MTTC KPI via SOAR-ISE measurements. That is important for the audit, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.*

---

## Description

We continuously evidence NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) MTTC KPI via SOAR-ISE measurements.

## Value

Quantitative MTTC is the hard NIS2 evidence — it shifts the audit from 'we have an IR plan' to 'we executed containment in N seconds'.

## Implementation

Daily.

## Evidence

Saved search archived to `audit_evidence` with NIS2 Art.21(2)(b) tagging.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

SOAR runs `block_endpoint` playbook. ISE 86001 within 30s. Healthy KPI.

### Negative scenario

Slow MTTC. Evidence captured for remediation.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-17.1.82 operational.
- Splunk SOAR 6.x+.

## Step 1-5

```ini
[UC-22.2.59: NIS2 — MTTC KPI Evidence]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.59,reg=NIS2,clause=Art.21(2)(b)
```

## SPL

```spl
index=phantom action_name=block_endpoint earliest=-1d
| eval soar_apply_time=_time
| stats earliest(soar_apply_time) as soar_t by container_id, mac_address
| join mac_address [search index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=86001 anc_action=quarantine | rename _time as ise_apply_time]
| eval mttc_seconds=ise_apply_time - soar_t
| stats avg(mttc_seconds) as avg_mttc, max(mttc_seconds) as max_mttc, count
| eval nis2_clause="Art.21(2)(b)", control="MTTC_KPI"
| collect index=audit_evidence
```

## Visualization

NIS2 MTTC dashboard.

## Known False Positives

1. SOAR action retries cause double counts.

## References

- [NIS2 Directive](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2022/2555/oj)
